seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gascoyne River and Gelorup. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gascoyne River vs Gelorup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gascoyne River and Gelorup.
heading: Gascoyne River vs Gelorup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gascoyne River, Western Australia and Gelorup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gascoyne River has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Gelorup (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gascoyne River or Gelorup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gascoyne River has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Gelorup scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gascoyne River scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Gelorup scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gascoyne River or Gelorup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gascoyne River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
